title: POH_CERTIFICATE
description: Proof of Human Involvement certificate issued by a Referee. Certifies the human/AI contribution ratio for a specified anchor range of a terminal's output. Designed to be attached to creative or data outputs to assert provenance. Compatible with C2PA (Coalition for Content Provenance and Authenticity) manifests: a C2PA assertion can reference cert_url to combine tool/environment provenance (C2PA) with human involvement ratio (RACKP).

issue_date.description: Datetime when this certificate was issued.

referee_id.description: Terminal ID of the Referee that issued this certificate.

subject_terminal_id.description: Terminal ID of the subject whose human involvement is certified.

anchor_range.description: The CLAIM_ANCHOR period evaluated for this certificate. Defined by the requesting application (e.g. per file save, per session, per project).

provenance.description: Human/AI contribution ratio assessed over the anchor_range.

confidence_level.description: Referee's confidence in the ratio assessment.

keeper.description: The Keeper that holds the Claimant's anchoring records used as the basis for this certificate. Enables independent verification of the anchor chain by any third party.

keeper_id.description: Terminal ID of the Keeper that holds the subject's CLAIM_ANCHOR records.

keeper_endpoint.description: Optional endpoint URL of the Keeper for direct chain verification queries.

subject_data_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the specific data artifact this certificate is issued for. Provided by the Claimant application at request time. Recipients can independently verify that this certificate corresponds to a given artifact by computing its hash and comparing against this field.

cert_url.description: Canonical URL where this certificate can be retrieved and independently verified. When integrating with C2PA, this URL can be referenced in a C2PA assertion to combine tool/environment provenance (C2PA) with human involvement ratio (RACKP).

proof_hash.description: SHA-256 of the canonical certificate body (all fields except proof_hash and signature, serialized per RFC 8785). Used to verify certificate integrity independently of the issuing Referee.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature by the issuing Referee over the canonical certificate body.
